metadata.annotator: Kimi AI-assisted (pending review by project lead)

company.name: Oracle Corporation
company.primary_assets: Servers, Network Equipment, Data Centers (owned + third-party operating-leased), Oracle Engineered Systems, Acquired Intangibles and Goodwill (Cerner etc.)
company.coverage_note: Oracle财年为6月1日至次年5月31日。本10-K覆盖FY2024（2023-06-01至2024-05-31），签署日期2024-06-20（HTML第15203行）。注意：Oracle的折旧年限变更发生在FY2023 Q1（4年延至5年），在FY2024 10-K的Note 1中作为既有会计估计再次披露；FY2024本期内无任何折旧年限变更。

financial_highlights.impairment_charges_note: NA - Note 1明确声明'We did not recognize any significant property impairment charges in fiscal 2024, 2023 or 2022'（HTML L7323）。MD&A两处（L1780、L3482）提及FY2024 acquisition related and other expenses（合计$314M）中包含'certain asset impairment charges'，但未单独量化，且非重大固定资产减值。
financial_highlights.finance_lease_note: NA - Note 10披露Oracle仅有经营租赁（'We have operating leases that primarily relate to certain of our data centers and facilities'，HTML L10623），无融资租赁。FY2024经营租赁费用$1.2B；已承诺但未上表的数据中心经营租赁$22.9B（FY2025-2027起租、9-15年期），期后又新签$9.3B（L10695）。
financial_highlights.server_depreciation_pct_note: NA - depreciation by asset class not disclosed

accounting_policy.leasehold_improvements: lesser of the estimated useful lives of the improvements or the lease terms
accounting_policy.change_effective: FY2023 Q1 (effective at the beginning of fiscal 2023, i.e., 2022-06-01)
accounting_policy.change_in_estimate_method: prospectively (change in accounting estimate applied to server carrying value as of May 31, 2022; no retrospective adjustment of prior periods)
accounting_policy.property_impairment_statement: We did not recognize any significant property impairment charges in fiscal 2024, 2023 or 2022
accounting_policy.policy_risk_note: ⚠️样本前提修正：任务外部信息称'Oracle FY2024将服务器折旧年限从5年延长至8年（全样本最极端）'，但FY2024 10-K正文（HTML L7317）实际披露的是FY2023 Q1将服务器年限从4年延长至5年、减少FY2023经营费用$434M；全文检索'eight years'仅命中员工任期（L761），无任何5→8年或FY2024本期年限变更披露。Note 5固定资产明细表（L7923-7925）显示'Computer, network, machinery and equipment'年限为1-5年，与5年上限一致。即便如此，方向性证据成立：Oracle在AI算力扩张期选择延长而非缩短服务器年限，未来适用不追溯；且Note 1声明连续三年无重大固定资产减值（L7323），与5年折旧年限+1-2年AI硬件迭代周期的错配共同构成本项目的核心风险画像。

risk_signals.source: Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements - Note 1. Organization and Significant Accounting Policies
risk_signals.keyword_matched: increased the estimate of the useful lives from four years to five years
risk_signals.text_excerpt: During the first quarter of fiscal 2023, we completed an assessment of the useful lives of our servers and increased the estimate of the useful lives from four years to five years effective at the beginning of fiscal 2023. Based on the carrying value of our servers as of May 31, 2022, this change in accounting estimate decreased our total operating expenses by $ 434 million during fiscal 2023.
risk_signals.page_location: Note 1. Organization and Significant Accounting Policies - Critical Accounting Policies (HTML line 7317)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: This is the only useful-life change disclosed in the FY2024 10-K and it is the exact behavior the project targets: Oracle LENGTHENED server depreciation life (4->5 years) effective FY2023, mechanically cutting FY2023 operating expenses by $434M (~5.1% of FY2023 net income of $8,503M), applied prospectively so prior under-depreciation is never corrected. Note: the change is dated FY2023 (not FY2024, and 4->5 years, not 5->8 years as assumed externally); in FY2024 no further change was made, but the extended 5-year life governs all server depreciation in FY2024.
evidence_chain: Official disclosure: servers 4->5 years, effective beginning FY2023, disclosed in FY2024 10-K Note 1
Quantified effect: -$434M total operating expenses during fiscal 2023 (prospectively, no restatement)
Change direction is lengthening (expense-reducing) precisely while Oracle was ramping AI/OCI infrastructure investment
AI hardware iteration cycle is 1-2 years (NVIDIA H100 2022 -> B100 2024); a 5-year life spans 2.5-5 technology generations
All servers on Oracle's books during FY2024 (computer/network/equipment gross $20,989M) are depreciated under the extended life
Result: FY2024 reported net income of $10,467M embeds the ongoing benefit of the extended server life; historical FY2022-and-earlier under-depreciation permanently uncorrected

risk_signals.source: Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements - Note 5. Property, Plant and Equipment
risk_signals.keyword_matched: Computer, network, machinery and equipment / 1 - 5 years
risk_signals.text_excerpt: Computer, network, machinery and equipment: Useful Life 1 - 5 years: $20,989 million (May 31, 2024) vs $17,258 million (May 31, 2023). Total property, plant and equipment: $34,818 million (2024) vs $28,674 million (2023); Accumulated depreciation: $(13,282) vs $(11,605); Total property, plant and equipment, net: $21,536 vs $17,069.
risk_signals.page_location: Note 5. Property, Plant and Equipment (HTML lines 7888, 7923-8023); policy text at Note 1 (line 7323): 'Depreciation is computed using the straight-line method based on estimated useful lives of the assets, which range from one to 40 years.'
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Post-change official schedule: the fastest-obsolescing asset class (computer, network, machinery and equipment - which includes OCI servers) is depreciated straight-line over at most 5 years. This asset class grew 21.6% YoY ($17,258M -> $20,989M gross) as Oracle built out AI cloud capacity, meaning a growing share of the asset base is subject to the life-mismatch risk.
evidence_chain: Computer/network/equipment gross: $20,989M at FY2024 end (+21.6% YoY), depreciated over 1-5 years
Total PP&E gross grew $34,818M from $28,674M (+21.4%); net PP&E up 26.2% to $21,536M
Server life (5 years) vs AI accelerator economic cycle (1-2 years) = 2.5-5x mismatch
Accumulated depreciation ($13,282M) = only 38.1% of gross PP&E, consistent with a young, rapidly expanding asset base
Result: the newest, largest asset cohort is depreciated under the most optimistic life assumption

risk_signals.source: Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements - Note 1. Property, Plant and Equipment
risk_signals.keyword_matched: We did not recognize any significant property impairment charges
risk_signals.text_excerpt: Property, plant and equipment are periodically reviewed for impairment whenever events or changes in circumstances indicate that the carrying amount of an asset may not be recoverable at an appropriate asset or asset group level. We did not recognize any significant property impairment charges in fiscal 2024, 2023 or 2022.
risk_signals.page_location: Note 1. Organization and Significant Accounting Policies - Property, Plant and Equipment (HTML line 7323)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Three consecutive fiscal years with zero significant property impairment, spanning the entire generative-AI hardware upheaval (ChatGPT Nov 2022 onward). Under the asset-group undiscounted-cash-flow test, server obsolescence is diluted by shared cloud revenue streams, so rapid technology turnover never surfaces on the income statement - the depreciation schedule (5 years) is the only charge, and it was lengthened, not shortened.
evidence_chain: Impairment test triggered only by 'events or changes in circumstances', measured at asset-group level
FY2022-FY2024: no significant property impairment despite AI hardware generation turnover
Same period: server life extended 4->5 years (expense down), never shortened
Accounting system is asymmetric: good news (longer life) recognized immediately, bad news (obsolescence) absorbed silently
Result: FY2024 margins are structurally flattered relative to economic consumption of the server fleet

risk_signals.source: Item 1A. Risk Factors - Data Center Capacity; Note 10. Leases
risk_signals.keyword_matched: long-term operating lease commitments with third-party data center providers
risk_signals.text_excerpt: we entered, and expect to continue to enter, into long-term operating lease commitments with third-party data center providers that generally require us to pay significant contract termination fees to early exit such obligations should our strategies change, which could adversely impact our profitability and cash flows. ... As of May 31, 2024, we have $22.9 billion of additional operating lease commitments, primarily for data centers, that are generally expected to commence between fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2027 and for terms of nine to fifteen years that were not reflected on our consolidated balance sheet as of May 31, 2024. ... Subsequent to May 31, 2024, we entered into $9.3 billion of additional operating lease commitments for data centers.
risk_signals.page_location: Item 1A. Risk Factors (HTML line 894); Note 10. Leases, Other Commitments and Certain Contingencies (HTML lines 10623, 10695)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Oracle is committing $32.2B ($22.9B + $9.3B) of OFF-balance-sheet, 9-15 year data center lease commitments on top of owned PP&E - an enormous bet on multi-year AI cloud demand. If AI demand or architecture shifts, Oracle faces either lease termination fees or stranded capacity; owned servers inside those facilities would simultaneously lose economic value far faster than the 5-year depreciation schedule assumes.
evidence_chain: Off-BS data center lease commitments: $22.9B (FY2025-2027 commencement, 9-15 year terms) + $9.3B signed after year-end
Owned PP&E net is only $21.5B - committed leases exceed the entire owned fixed-asset base
Lease terms (9-15 years) far exceed server tech cycle (1-2 years); facility commitments outlive multiple hardware generations
Early exit triggers 'significant contract termination fees' = abandonment risk explicitly disclosed
Result: capacity-planning errors would convert directly into impairment/termination charges, revealing under-depreciation of the associated infrastructure

risk_signals.text_excerpt: we have in some instances responded to such shortages by committing to higher purchases and balances of hardware products that we market and sell to our customers and that we use as a part of our cloud infrastructure to deliver our cloud offerings, relative to our historical positions. While this permits us to secure manufacturing capacity, it has increased excess and obsolescence risk of such hardware products and could adversely impact our profitability and cash flows. We expect these factors will continue to impact us in the future.
risk_signals.page_location: Item 1A. Risk Factors - Supply Chain (HTML line 911)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Management's own use of the word 'obsolescence' for hardware that feeds its cloud infrastructure. Oracle over-committed hardware purchases during supply shortages; these components/servers face excess-and-obsolescence risk, i.e., economic life shorter than procurement assumptions - the same direction of bias as the extended depreciation life.
evidence_chain: Oracle committed to above-historical hardware purchase volumes for cloud infrastructure
Management explicitly flags 'excess and obsolescence risk' of such hardware
Obsolescence risk acknowledged in Risk Factors but absent from depreciation assumptions (5-year life unchanged in FY2024)
Result: risk disclosures and accounting estimates are internally inconsistent - the 10-K admits faster obsolescence while depreciating slower

risk_signals.keyword_matched: AI technology and services are highly competitive and rapidly evolving
risk_signals.text_excerpt: Machine learning and AI, including generative AI, are increasingly driving innovations in technology, and AI technology and services are highly competitive and rapidly evolving. We have invested, and expect to continue to invest, significant resources to build and support our AI products, and if our AI products fail to operate as anticipated or as well as competing products ... we may fail to recoup our investments in AI and our business and reputation may be harmed.
risk_signals.page_location: Item 1A. Risk Factors - AI Products (HTML line 887)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Oracle describes its AI environment as 'rapidly evolving' and warns it 'may fail to recoup' AI investments. Investments in AI products are embodied in the OCI server fleet depreciated over 5 years; management itself concedes the payback period is uncertain while the accounting life is fixed and lengthened.
evidence_chain: AI tech 'highly competitive and rapidly evolving' per management
Significant ongoing AI investment with uncertain recoupment
Recoupment risk = economic life risk of the underlying compute assets
5-year depreciation life assumes stable recoverability the Risk Factors contradict
Result: accounting life exceeds competitively-defensible economic life of AI compute assets

risk_signals.source: MD&A - Total Revenues and Operating Expenses / Acquisition Related and Other Expenses
risk_signals.text_excerpt: higher acquisition related and other expenses, which were primarily due to certain asset impairment charges and certain litigation related charges. ... On a constant currency basis, acquisition related and other expenses increased in fiscal 2024 relative to fiscal 2023 due to higher other expenses primarily related to certain asset impairment charges and certain litigation related charges.
risk_signals.page_location: Item 7. MD&A (HTML lines 1780, 3482); acquisition related and other expense line: $314M FY2024 vs $190M FY2023 (income statement, lines 5214-5225)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: FY2024 did contain actual asset impairment charges (amount not separately disclosed, embedded in the $314M acquisition-related-and-other line, up $124M YoY). Although Note 1 states they were not 'significant property' impairments, their existence demonstrates that write-downs continue to occur outside the depreciation schedule.
evidence_chain: FY2024 acquisition related and other expenses rose $190M -> $314M, driven partly by 'certain asset impairment charges'
Impairment amount not separately quantified (immaterial individually, per Note 1 'no significant property impairment')
Pattern: small recurring impairments + zero schedule adjustment = depreciation assumptions never updated downward
Result: income statement absorbs obsolescence only in small ad-hoc charges, while the 5-year schedule stays intact

risk_signals.source: Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows; Note 5 - Construction in Progress
risk_signals.keyword_matched: Depreciation / Capital expenditures / Construction in progress
risk_signals.text_excerpt: Depreciation: $3,129 (FY2024), $2,526 (FY2023), $1,972 (FY2022). Capital expenditures: $(6,866) (FY2024), $(8,695) (FY2023), $(4,511) (FY2022). Construction in progress: $5,634 (May 31, 2024) vs $3,846 (May 31, 2023). (1) Amounts primarily consist of computer equipment to be built and deployed at our data centers.
risk_signals.page_location: Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows (HTML lines 6765-6776, 6999-7010); Note 5 (HTML lines 7975-7984, footnote at line 8026)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Construction in progress - explicitly 'computer equipment to be built and deployed at our data centers' - ballooned 46.5% to $5,634M, i.e., a huge cohort of servers not yet depreciating. FY2024 depreciation ($3,129M) was only 45.6% of FY2024 capex; combined with $32.2B of new data center lease commitments, the depreciation line will step up sharply in FY2025-2027 - or will be suppressed if lives are extended again.
evidence_chain: CIP (data center computer equipment) up 46.5% YoY to $5,634M - none of it depreciating yet
FY2024 depreciation $3,129M = 45.6% of FY2024 capex $6,866M
Two-year capex (FY2023+FY2024 = $15.6B) far exceeds FY2024 depreciation, guaranteeing a future expense step-up
If management repeats the FY2023 playbook (extend lives), the step-up can be deferred and margins further flattered
Result: current depreciation understates the run-rate cost of the AI capacity being deployed

dimension_scores.dimension_name: 折旧年限 vs 技术实际寿命
dimension_scores.dimension_name_en: Depreciation Life vs Technology Useful Life
dimension_scores.reasoning: 服务器折旧年限经FY2023 Q1变更后现行5年（Note 5明细表'Computer, network, machinery and equipment 1-5年'，L7923-7925），落在锚点4-6年档→4分。⚠️前提修正：外部信息所称'FY2024延至8年'在10-K中不存在（全文'eight years'仅指员工任期L761），实际为FY2023由4年延至5年。对照AI硬件1-2年迭代周期（NVIDIA H100 2022→B100 2024），错配比2.5-5倍。公司自身在Risk Factors承认AI'rapidly evolving'（L887）与硬件'obsolescence risk'（L911），年限与经济现实方向相反。

key_metrics.premise_correction: 外部假设的5→8年/FY2024变更不成立；实际为FY2023 Q1 4→5年

dimension_scores.reasoning: 锚点'历史有延长→4'：FY2023 Q1将服务器年限4→5年、按未来适用法处理（基于2022-05-31账面价值、不追溯），减少FY2023经营费用$434M（约占FY2023净利润$8,503M的5.1%），该估计变更的持续效应贯穿FY2024全部服务器折旧。FY2024本期无进一步变更（故不到5分），但历史变更方向为延长（利己），且连续三年无重大固定资产减值（L7323），估计裁量呈单向特征。

FY2024有实际减值但很小：MD&A两处（L1780、L3482）披露acquisition related and other费用（$314M）含'certain asset impairment charges'，未单独量化；Note 1明确声明FY2024/2023/2022连续三年无重大固定资产减值（L7323）。直接信号存在（固定资产减值政策L7323、收购减值风险L931、数据中心容量/终止费风险L894、供应链过时风险L911），但多数为政策性与非PP&E，缺乏Meta（$2.43B）式的大额实际减值。介于'有实际减值或≥3处直接信号→4'与'仅间接→3'之间，因实际减值不重大且明确非property级，取3分。

key_metrics.unquantified_asset_impairment_charges: within acquisition related and other expenses of $314M (FY2024)

dimension_scores.reasoning: CAPEX/收入=6,866/52,961=13.0%，落在8-15%档→3分（FY2023为8,695/49,954=17.4%，FY2024表观回落）。但需注意结构性低估：Oracle大量数据中心容量通过经营租赁获得（FY2024租赁费$1.2B，另有$22.9B未上表承诺+期后$9.3B新签），真实基础设施投入强度远高于现金流量表CAPEX所显示；在建工程（主要为数据中心计算机设备）同比+46.5%至$5,634M，FY2025-2027折旧阶梯式上升已成定局。

Oracle以OCI直接运营AI云基础设施，是算力竞赛主力之一：承接大规模AI训练/推理负载（与NVIDIA深度合作、参股ARM服务器芯片商Ampere约29%），管理层自述AI'highly competitive and rapidly evolving'且投入'重大资源'（L887），并以$32.2B数据中心租赁承诺+在建工程+46.5%激进扩产。竞争对手（Microsoft/Google/Amazon/Meta）同步军备竞赛迫使硬件1-2年持续升级。但Oracle自有基础模型暴露弱于Google/Meta（更多以基础设施服务商身份参与），评4分。

key_metrics.ai_role: OCI hyperscaler + ~29% stake in Ampere Computing (ARM server chips, related party)

composite_score.confidence_reason: 全部财务数据与引文逐字核对于FY2024 10-K正文（含行号）。前提修正已落实：外部假设的'FY2024 5→8年'在文件中不存在，实际为FY2023 Q1 4→5年（-$434M费用，L7317），本标注按文件事实评分。扣分项：FY2024本期无年限变更、无重大PP&E减值，故D2/D3不及GOOGL/META；加分项：$32.2B表外数据中心租赁承诺与在建工程+46.5%显示风险正在向后累积。

peer_comparison.note: CONFIRMED (project lead, 2025-07-15). Servers 4-5 years, realized $2.43B facilities-consolidation impairment in 2023. ORCL (3.6) lower: no significant PP&E impairment history and smaller realized write-downs.

DRAFT (2026-07-20). Servers 4->6 years effective FY2023 with -$3.9B depreciation / +$3.0B net income quantified in three locations. ORCL's change (4->5 years, -$434M) is milder in both life and magnitude.
Estimated. Also extended server useful lives (4->6 years, effective FY2023) amid Azure AI build-out; similar prospectively-applied change-in-estimate behavior, pending annotation.
Estimated. Sells GPUs rather than operating hyperscale fleets; manufacturing-equipment asset structure; different depreciation risk profile.
Estimated. Fabless chip designer; asset-light relative to cloud operators; pending annotation.

comparative_context.oracle_positioning_note: ORCL综合3.6（中高风险），低于GOOGL/META的主要原因：年限变更幅度较小（4→5 vs 4→6/6年档）、FY2024本期无变更、连续三年无重大PP&E减值。但Oracle是本批样本中表外容量承诺最激进者（$32.2B租赁承诺>全部自有PP&E净值$21.5B），风险呈现'向后递延'特征，FY2025-2027重估时应重点跟踪其服务器年限是否再次延长及减值确认。

annotation_methodology.source_document: Oracle Corporation Fiscal 2024 Annual Report on Form 10-K (CIK 0001341439, fiscal year ended May 31, 2024, signed June 20, 2024)
annotation_methodology.text_extraction_method: HTML parsing with Python (regex tag-stripping, encoding errors replaced) + keyword search (useful life/lives, depreciation, impairment, obsolescence, prospectively, change in estimate, eight years, technological advancement); every candidate hit manually verified in visible body text with HTML line numbers; hidden ix:hidden XBRL tag matches excluded
annotation_methodology.premise_verification: Task premise ('FY2024 server life extended 5->8 years') was explicitly tested against the filing:全文'eight years'唯一命中为员工任期（L761）；唯一年限变更为FY2023 Q1 4->5年（L7317）；Note 5固定资产表上限5年（L7923-7925）。标注以文件事实为准，偏差已在policy_risk_note与D1 reasoning中记录。
annotation_methodology.scoring_method: Five-dimension weighted scoring (D1 0.25 / D2 0.20 / D3 0.20 / D4 0.20 / D5 0.15), 1-5 integer scale per dimension, anchored to confirmed META 2023 benchmark (4.0) and GOOGL 2023 draft (4.25)
annotation_methodology.human_verification: All quoted text excerpts and financial figures manually verified against original 10-K filing at cited HTML line numbers
annotation_methodology.limitation: Single-year 10-K analysis. Oracle discloses no depreciation by asset class, no finance leases, and does not quantify the FY2024 'certain asset impairment charges' (marked NA). Much of Oracle's AI capacity is procured via operating leases (off-balance-sheet), so capex-based ratios understate true infrastructure intensity; FY2023 10-K cross-check for the original 4->5 year change context is pending.
